rationale: The citation is used in the methodology section for specific modeling choices, not just as background. The authors explicitly follow Zhang and Breugelmans (2012) when specifying latent-class segment membership with concomitant variables and when allowing correlation between spending and store-set choice errors. This indicates direct methodological borrowing that affects model construction, even though the application domain is different.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Zhang and Breugelmans (2012) as a methodological precedent for parts of its econometric specification, especially latent-class membership modeling and correlated error structure across decision layers. It applies these techniques to a different problem setting, namely hard-discounter entry and multiple-store shopping behavior.
